content.full_text:
Novoa
Jewelers
Since 1973

Bob & Patty Novoa

April 14, 1990

Fair Political Practice Commission
428 J. St.
Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA    95804

Mr. Jonathan Rothman
Counsel Legal Division FPPC

Dear Mr. Rothman,

     In regards to our recent conversation pertaining to a
possible conflict of interest under section 18703.5, we have
enclosed a copy of the agenda item listing the owners and
applicants in this action.

     Mr. and Mrs. Doug Stephens and Stephens Realtors are property
owners in this action being taken by the planning commission
and are also representing The Pratt Company as real estate
brokers for this project.

     Mr. and Mrs. Doug Stephens and Stephens Realtors have
maintained a personal as well as business open charge account
with our firm for a number of years.  During any given 12 month
period, purchases have exceeded one tenth of one percent of our
gross sales.

     In addition, one of my employees, Mrs. Harold Campos,
is also listed as a property owner.  I do not believe any conflict
exists from her employment, but nevertheless, I would appreciate
a ruling on this matter.

     If any further information is required, do not hesitate
to contact us.

                                   With my sincere appreciation,


                                   _______________________

                                   Robert G. Novoa
                                   Planning Commissioner
                                   City of Newman

                                   Enclosure


940 Fresno Street  •  Newman, CA 95360  •  (209) 862-2680

[The image appears to be a blank or nearly blank page with a light beige/cream colored background. There is a horizontal line visible roughly in the middle of the page, but no legible text content is present to transcribe.]

AGENDA ITEM NO._8_

PLANNING COMMISSION
CITY OF NEWMAN

February 15, 1990

STAFF REPORT

REQUEST: GENERAL PLAN CHANGE, REZONING, PREZONING AND ANNEXATION
SOUTH "N" STREET ANNEXATION
(Westside Marketplace)

APPLICATION INFORMATION

Owners: Douglas Stephens, Lyle Davis, Bernardino
Teixeira, Joan Wilkinson, Thomas DiMare,
Robert Bagley & Harold Campos
Applicants: The Pratt Company
Agent: None
Location: "N" Street (Highway 33), south of Inyo
Assessors Parcel: 26-16-2, 3, 7, 8, 30 & 31;
128-25-03, 09, 11, 12 & 13
Area of Parcels: 13.7 acres
Existing Zoning: R-1, and A-2-10, Exclusive Agriculture,
Urban Transition
Requested Zoning: C-2, General and Service Commercial and
R-1, Single Family Residential
General Plan: Low Density Residential
Flood Zone: AH, flooding depth of 1 to 3 feet
Present Land Use: Agriculture and ranchettes
Surrounding Land Use: Agriculture to the south, commercial to
the north and east, residential to the
west
Environmental Review: Negative Declaration recommended

ANALYSIS

The applicant proposes to construct an 11 acre shopping
center and develop 3 acres into residential lots. A portion of
the land is currently within the city limits and is zoned C-2. A
general plan change and rezoning to C-2 is requested for a 1.75
acre parcel now within the city limits and zoned R-1.

For the portion of the project which requires annexation,
the current general plan designation is low density residential.
A change in the general plan to commercial and prezoning to C-2
is requested for about 6.8 acres and prezoning to R-1 for the
remainder. All of this is shown on the attached map.

The shopping center is proposed to have about 117,000 square
feet of building area and will include a grocery store and drug
store with about 35,000 square feet each, 37,000 square feet of

I can see this image is mostly blank/white with some very faint yellow or orange markings in the center area, but the text is too illegible for me to accurately transcribe. The markings appear to be degraded or faded to the point where I cannot make out any clear words or content.

[illegible]

sections.analysis:
The applicant proposes to construct an 11 acre shopping center and develop 3 acres into residential lots. A portion of the land is currently within the city limits and is zoned C-2. A general plan change and rezoning to C-2 is requested for a 1.75 acre parcel now within the city limits and zoned R-1.

For the portion of the project which requires annexation, the current general plan designation is low density residential. A change in the general plan to commercial and prezzoning to C-2 is requested for about 6.8 acres and prezzoning to R-1 for the remainder. All of this is shown on the attached map.

The shopping center is proposed to have about 117,000 square feet of building area and will include a grocery store and drug store with about 35,000 square feet each, 37,000 square feet of

Mr. and Mrs. Doug Stephens and Stephens Realtors are property owners in this action being taken by the planning commission and are also representing The Pratt Company as real estate brokers for this project. Mr. and Mrs. Doug Stephens and Stephens Realtors have maintained a personal as well as business open charge account with our firm for a number of years. During any given 12 month period, purchases have exceeded one tenth of one percent of our gross sales. In addition, one of my employees, Mrs. Harold Campos, is also listed as a property owner.
